name.text: Session 2 Flower Summary and Petal Area 2 Submission

questiontext.text:
Submit the complete Session 2 code for the flower variables practice.

Your submitted program should include the variables and print statements from the tutorial, including the second flower with the _2 suffix.

For grading, the program output must include these required lines:

Sepal Length: 5.1
Sepal Width: 3.5
Petal Length: 1.4
Petal Width: 0.2
Species: setosa
petal_length_text: 1.4 | type: <class 'str'>
threshold_text: 2.0 | type: <class 'str'>
threshold_number: 2.0 | type: <class 'float'>
Petal Area 2: 0.27999999999999997

You may print other lines from the tutorial as well, but your program must include these exact required lines.

generalfeedback.text:
Common mistakes for this submission include:

  • Changing variable names that later sessions depend on, such as sepal_length, petal_length_text, or petal_area_2
  • Using the wrong values for the second flower variables
  • Forgetting to calculate petal_area_2 = petal_length_2 * petal_width_2
  • Printing a different label instead of the exact labels required by the tutorial
  • Skipping the conversion output lines or printing the wrong Python types
  • Removing the final print statement for petal_area_2

question.answerpreload:
"""Session 2: practice variables, basic arithmetic, comparisons, type conversions, and adding a second flower. Note: Variable names must match exactly because later sessions import these names. """
# Write your full Session 2 code below. # Keep the required variable names exactly as shown in the tutorial. # Make sure your program prints the required tutorial lines, # including the conversion lines and: # Petal Area 2: 0.27999999999999997
question.template:
import contextlib import io import json
student_code = """{{ STUDENT_ANSWER | e('py') }}""" stdout_buffer = io.StringIO() checks = [ ("Sepal Length output", "Sepal Length: 5.1", 1.0), ("Sepal Width output", "Sepal Width: 3.5", 1.0), ("Petal Length output", "Petal Length: 1.4", 1.0), ("Petal Width output", "Petal Width: 0.2", 1.0), ("Species output", "Species: setosa", 1.0), ("petal_length_text output", "petal_length_text: 1.4 | type: <class 'str'>", 1.0), ("threshold_text output", "threshold_text: 2.0 | type: <class 'str'>", 1.0), ("threshold_number output", "threshold_number: 2.0 | type: <class 'float'>", 1.0), ("Petal Area 2 output", "Petal Area 2: 0.27999999999999997", 2.0), ]
try: compiled = compile(student_code, "<student>", "exec") with contextlib.redirect_stdout(stdout_buffer): exec(compiled, {"__name__": "__main__"})
output_lines = stdout_buffer.getvalue().splitlines() rows = [[ "Check", "Expected", "Got", "Comment", "Mark", "iscorrect" ]] total_awarded = 0.0
for label, expected_line, mark in checks: if expected_line in output_lines: got = expected_line comment = "Required output line found." awarded = mark iscorrect = 1 else: got = output_lines[-1] if output_lines else "(no output)" comment = "Required output line not found." awarded = 0.0 iscorrect = 0
total_awarded += awarded rows.append([label, expected_line, got, comment, f"{awarded:.1f}/{mark:.1f}", iscorrect])
fraction = total_awarded / 10.0 except Exception as exc: rows = [[ "Check", "Expected", "Got", "Comment", "Mark", "iscorrect" ], [ "Program execution", "Program runs successfully and prints all required lines", f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}", "Your program did not run successfully.", "0.0/10.0", 0 ]] fraction = 0.0
print(json.dumps({ "fraction": fraction, "testresults": rows }))
question.answer:
"""Session 2: practice variables, basic arithmetic, comparisons, type conversions, and adding a second flower. Note: Variable names must match exactly because later sessions import these names. """
# Task 1: Print student summary (optional but good practice) id = "flower1" print("\n=== Flower Summary ===") print("ID:", id) # Task 2: Declare variables for one Iris flower # We define these variables to represent a single setosa flower.
sepal_length = 5.1 sepal_width = 3.5 petal_length = 1.4 petal_width = 0.2 species = "setosa"
print("\n=== Flower Summary ===") print("ID:", id) print("Sepal Length:", sepal_length) print("Sepal Width:", sepal_width) print("Petal Length:", petal_length) print("Petal Width:", petal_width) print("Species:", species)
# Task 3: Compute petal area # Multiply petal_length and petal_width to get the area petal_area = petal_length * petal_width # Observe the exact floating-point output print("\nPetal Area:", petal_area)
# Task 4: Define rule variables and comparison # We use these variables in the next session for our rule-based classifier THRESHOLD = 2.0 FEATURE_NAME = "petal_length" POSITIVE_LABEL = "setosa" NEGATIVE_LABEL = "not_setosa" LABEL_KEY = "species"
is_short_petal = petal_length < THRESHOLD print("\n=== Comparison ===") print("is_short_petal (petal_length < threshold):", is_short_petal)
# Task 5: Type conversion practice petal_length_text = str(petal_length) threshold_text = "2.0" threshold_number = float(threshold_text)
print("\n=== Conversions ===") print("petal_length_text:", petal_length_text, "| type:", type(petal_length_text)) print("threshold_text:", threshold_text, "| type:", type(threshold_text)) print("threshold_number:", threshold_number, "| type:", type(threshold_number))
# Task 6: Add a second flower using _2 suffix # It is important to keep the _2 suffix so Session 3 can convert them into a dictionary sepal_length_2 = 4.9 sepal_width_2 = 3.0 petal_length_2 = 1.4 petal_width_2 = 0.2 species_2 = "setosa"
petal_area_2 = petal_length_2 * petal_width_2 print("\n=== Flower 2 ===") print("Petal Area 2:", petal_area_2)

testcode.text: Check that the program prints all required Session 2 output lines.
